task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the OrientDB Studio web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'http://localhost:2480/studio/index.html' (or similar localhost:2480 URL) and the OrientDB Studio login/connection page is visible.

task_completion.description: The agent opened the 'New DB' or 'Create Database' dialog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, popup, or form specifically for creating a new database is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The agent entered 'LibraryDB' as the new database name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'LibraryDB' is visible inside the 'Name' or 'Database Name' input field of the creation form.

task_completion.description: The agent selected 'plocal' as the storage type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The storage type dropdown or selection field shows 'plocal' as the chosen option.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the server credentials to authorize the creation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The server username field contains 'root'. The password field will likely show masked characters (dots/asterisks) corresponding to 'GymAnything123!'.

task_completion.description: The database 'LibraryDB' was successfully created and is visible in the interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'LibraryDB' appears in the list of available databases on the connection screen, or the interface shows the dashboard/schema page actively connected to 'LibraryDB'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OrientDB Studio web GUI to create the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the web browser and OrientDB Studio interface. There should be no terminal windows using 'curl' or the OrientDB console script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the creation form.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate steps like typing in fields, opening dropdowns (like the storage type dropdown), and clicking the create button.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the database presence.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open to manipulate the DOM to falsely display 'LibraryDB' in the database list.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OrientDB Community Edition 3.2.36 is running at http://localhost:2480. The task requires creating a database via the Studio UI. The target database name is 'LibraryDB' (case-insensitive for backend verification, but should be typed as requested). The required storage type is 'plocal' (Paginated Local Storage). The server credentials required for creation are username 'root' and password 'GymAnything123!'. Success is visually confirmed when 'LibraryDB' appears in the Studio's database list or when the Studio successfully connects to the newly created 'LibraryDB' dashboard.
